description_long: This object is a sculpted stone head, reminiscent of classical Greco-Roman busts, featuring intricately carved curly hair and a full beard. The facial features are stylized, with a prominent nose, deep-set eyes, and a stern expression. The hair is voluminous and detailed, with layered curls that wrap around the head and extend to the nape of the neck. The stone surface is smooth but shows subtle chisel marks and natural variations in tone, suggesting it is carved from marble or a similar material. The back of the head is rounded and fully covered in textured curls, while the front displays the face in high relief. This type of object would typically serve as a decorative or commemorative barrier in an urban park, plaza, or near a historical building.
description: A detailed stone bust with curly hair and a bearded face, carved in a classical style. The surface is smooth with intricate hair texture and stylized facial features.
description_view_0: The left rear side of the head, showing the layered curls and partial profile of the face.
description_view_1: The back of the head, dominated by dense, rounded curls with no facial features visible.
description_view_2: The right rear side, highlighting the hair texture and a partial view of the face and beard.
description_view_3: The front view, displaying the full face with deep-set eyes, prominent nose, and detailed beard.
attribute_location: urban park or plaza near historical monuments
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on a pedestal or low wall
attribute_pattern: Highly regular, sculpted curls and facial features with subtle chisel marks; no natural veining or mottling visible.
attribute_weathering: Smooth and polished with minimal signs of weathering, indicating recent carving or sheltered placement.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts sharply with natural elements and modern structures, typically serving as a focal point or decorative barrier in a landscaped or paved area.
